paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs
summary: Question 6.4 asks whether every orientation of $C_4$ is Eulerian-avoidable, i.e., whether for every orientation $F$ of $C_4$ there exists a function $d_F$ such that every Eulerian digraph of minimum out-degree at least $d_F(k)$ contains an $F$-free subdigraph of minimum out-degree at least $k$. The source paper (arXiv:2510.11311) shows that the anti-directed orientation of $C_4$ (which is the complete bipartite digraph $\vec{K}_{2,2}$) is not avoidable in the general setting, but leaves the Eulerian-avoidable status of all orientations of $C_4$ open. No follow-up work addressing this specific question was found in a web search conducted in May 2026.
search_queries: Eulerian-avoidable orientation C4 directed graphs Thomassen conjecture 2510.11311
Eulerian-avoidable digraph C4 orientation 2025 2026 graph theory
Christoph Janzer Petrova Steiner "Thomassen" directed graphs avoidable 2025 2026 arXiv
notes: No follow-up found. The paper establishes the general (non-Eulerian) framework and shows the anti-directed C4 is not avoidable, but Question 6.4 specifically asks about the Eulerian-restricted setting, which is entirely open. The conjecture is very recent (October 2025) and the absence of follow-up is expected.
